title: FSM synth unexpected results
body:
Hi,

Doing some synth with Yosys version 0.59+134.

Please consider this FSM of a sequence detector (with overlapping) given below

```
module sequence_1010(
    input clk,
    input res_n,
    input din,
    output wire dout
);
    parameter pass = 4'b1010;
    localparam S0 = 0;
    localparam S1 = 1;
    localparam S2 = 2;
    localparam S3 = 3;
    (* fsm_encoding = "auto" *) reg [1:0] state, next_state;

    // State update. Reset is async.
    always @(posedge clk or negedge res_n) begin
        if (!res_n)
            state <= S0;
        else
            state <= next_state;
    end

    assign dout = (state == S3 && din === pass[0]) ? 1 : 0;

    always @(*) begin
        case (state)
            S0:
                next_state = din === pass[3] ? S1 : S0;
            S1:
                next_state = din === pass[2] ? S2 : S1;
            S2:
                next_state = din === pass[1] ? S3 : S0;
            S3:
                next_state = din === pass[0] ? S2 : S1; // overlapping
            default:
                next_state = S0;
        endcase
    end
endmodule
```

The synth is done as follows:

`yosys -p "read_verilog sequence.v; hierarchy -check -top sequence_1010; proc; opt -nodffe -nosdff; clean; opt_share; opt_muxtree; clean; fsm -nomap; fsm_export -o demo.kiss2; stat; show"
`

Stat results:
```
8. Printing statistics.

=== sequence_1010 ===

        +----------Local Count, excluding submodules.
        | 
       18 wires
      144 wire bits
        6 public wires
        8 public wire bits
        4 ports
        4 port bits
       14 cells
        1   $adff
        3   $eq
        2   $eqx
        1   $logic_and
        1   $logic_not
        4   $mux
        1   $pmux
        1   $reduce_or
```

FSM output:
```
4. Executing FSM pass (extract and optimize FSM).

4.1. Executing FSM_DETECT pass (finding FSMs in design).
Warning: Regarding the user-specified fsm_encoding attribute on sequence_1010.next_state:
    Users of state reg look like FSM recoding might result in larger circuit.
    Doesn't look like a proper FSM. Possible simulation-synthesis mismatch!
FSM state register sequence_1010.state already has fsm_encoding attribute.

4.2. Executing FSM_EXTRACT pass (extracting FSM from design).
Extracting FSM `\next_state' from module `\sequence_1010'.
  root of input selection tree: 2'mm
  fsm extraction failed: incomplete input selection tree root.
Extracting FSM `\state' from module `\sequence_1010'.
  found $adff cell for state register: $procdff$25
  root of input selection tree: \next_state
  found reset state: 2'00 (from async reset)
  found ctrl input: $auto$opt_reduce.cc:137:opt_pmux$27
  found ctrl input: $procmux$18_CMP
  found ctrl input: $procmux$20_CMP
  found ctrl input: $eqx$test.v:23$4_Y
  found state code: 2'01
  found state code: 2'10
  found ctrl input: $eqx$test.v:29$8_Y
  found state code: 2'11
  found ctrl output: $eq$test.v:23$3_Y
  found ctrl output: $procmux$18_CMP
  found ctrl output: $procmux$19_CMP
  found ctrl output: $procmux$20_CMP
  ctrl inputs: { $eqx$test.v:29$8_Y $eqx$test.v:23$4_Y $auto$opt_reduce.cc:137:opt_pmux$27 }
  ctrl outputs: { $procmux$20_CMP $procmux$19_CMP $procmux$18_CMP $eq$test.v:23$3_Y \next_state }
  transition:       2'00 3'0-- ->       2'00 6'100000
  transition:       2'00 3'1-- ->       2'01 6'100001
  transition:       2'10 3'0-- ->       2'00 6'001000
  transition:       2'10 3'1-- ->       2'11 6'001011
  transition:       2'01 3'-0- ->       2'01 6'010001
  transition:       2'01 3'-1- ->       2'10 6'010010
  transition:       2'11 3'-0- ->       2'01 6'000101
  transition:       2'11 3'-1- ->       2'10 6'000110

4.3. Executing FSM_OPT pass (simple optimizations of FSMs).
Optimizing FSM `$fsm$\state$28' from module `\sequence_1010'.
  Removing unused input signal $auto$opt_reduce.cc:137:opt_pmux$27.

4.4. Executing OPT_CLEAN pass (remove unused cells and wires).
Finding unused cells or wires in module \sequence_1010..
Removed 10 unused cells and 10 unused wires.
<suppressed ~11 debug messages>

4.5. Executing FSM_OPT pass (simple optimizations of FSMs).
Optimizing FSM `$fsm$\state$28' from module `\sequence_1010'.
  Removing unused output signal \next_state [0].
  Removing unused output signal \next_state [1].
  Removing unused output signal $procmux$18_CMP.
  Removing unused output signal $procmux$19_CMP.
  Removing unused output signal $procmux$20_CMP.

4.6. Executing FSM_RECODE pass (re-assigning FSM state encoding).
Recoding FSM `$fsm$\state$28' from module `\sequence_1010' using `auto' encoding:
  mapping auto encoding to `one-hot` for this FSM.
  00 -> ---1
  10 -> --1-
  01 -> -1--
  11 -> 1---

4.7. Executing FSM_INFO pass (dumping all available information on FSM cells).

FSM `$fsm$\state$28' from module `sequence_1010':
-------------------------------------

  Information on FSM $fsm$\state$28 (\state):

  Number of input signals:    2
  Number of output signals:   1
  Number of state bits:       4

  Input signals:
    0: $eqx$test.v:23$4_Y
    1: $eqx$test.v:29$8_Y

  Output signals:
    0: $eq$test.v:23$3_Y

  State encoding:
    0:     4'---1  <RESET STATE>
    1:     4'--1-
    2:     4'-1--
    3:     4'1---

  Transition Table (state_in, ctrl_in, state_out, ctrl_out):
      0:     0 2'0-   ->     0 1'0
      1:     0 2'1-   ->     2 1'0
      2:     1 2'0-   ->     0 1'0
      3:     1 2'1-   ->     3 1'0
      4:     2 2'-1   ->     1 1'0
      5:     2 2'-0   ->     2 1'0
      6:     3 2'-1   ->     1 1'1
      7:     3 2'-0   ->     2 1'1
```

demo.kiss2 output:
```
.i 2
.o 1
.p 8
.s 4
.r s0
0- s0 s0 0
1- s0 s2 0
0- s1 s0 0
1- s1 s3 0
-1 s2 s1 0
-0 s2 s2 0
-1 s3 s1 1
-0 s3 s2 1
```

Multiple issues observed:

1) stat results have 1 $adff. But there are 4 states, so 2 bits are needed to save the state. How come only 1 flip flop was synthesized?

2) Regarding the states:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38805e3e-96ab-4614-ae0d-b5ec51d54686)

2.1) Apparently, states S2 and S1 were switched (S2 should be S1), why does it happen?

2.2) Some strange transitions take place:
S1 (on the picture) to S3 happens when IN=1, but in verilog it is stated to be 0

2.3) Both S3 transitions have OUT=1, but that is not the case, OUT=0 when moving to S2 (S1 on the pic).

3) Why there is this message in FSM output?
`fsm extraction failed: incomplete input selection tree root.`

4) A usage question - when running without  -nomap in fsm command, the fsm_export command does not produce a kiss2 file, is it the correct behavior?

Is there a mistake somewhere? Please advice.
